steps.description: Disperse sodium molybdate dihydrate (molybdenum precursor) in distilled water at a molar ratio of sodium molybdate to thiourea of 1:4.
Add thiourea to the molybdenum precursor solution at a ratio of 1:4 (molybdenum precursor:thiourea) and agitate for 15 minutes to ensure homogeneity.
Transfer the homogenized mixture into a Teflon-coated stainless-steel autoclave and heat to 200°C for 22 hours.
Cool the autoclave to room temperature to allow the synthesized material to solidify and form crystals.
Remove the powders from the autoclave and dry at 80°C for 8 hours in an oven to remove residual water and impurities.

synthesis.notes: The molar ratio of sodium molybdate dihydrate to thiourea was 1:4. The synthesized MoS2 exhibited hexagonal 2H-MoS2 phase confirmed by XRD peaks at 2θ values of 13.73°, 31.38°, 32.84°, and 56.59° corresponding to (002), (100), (101), and (110) planes (JCPDS card number 37-1492). Raman spectroscopy showed E1 2g and A1g modes at 368.96 cm−1 and 398.36 cm−1.

steps.description: Disperse the molybdenum precursor (sodium molybdate dihydrate) in distilled water at a molar ratio of molybdenum precursor to thiourea of 1:4, along with graphene oxide (GO) as a co-catalyst.

Transfer the homogenized mixture into a Teflon-coated stainless-steel autoclave and heat to 200°C for 22 hours to carry out the hydrothermal reaction, reducing GO to rGO and forming MoS2-rGO composite.
Cool the autoclave to room temperature to allow the synthesized material to solidify and form crystals.
Remove the powders from the autoclave and dry at 80°C for 8 hours in an oven to remove residual water and impurities.

The mixture (with GO added for the composite) was placed in a Teflon-coated stainless-steel autoclave and heated.
The autoclave was cooled to room temperature.
The powders were removed and dried in an oven.

steps.description: Dispersion of sodium molybdate dihydrate (molybdenum precursor) in distilled water
Addition of thiourea to the molybdenum precursor solution at a molar ratio of 1:4 (Mo:thiourea), along with graphene oxide. The mixture was agitated for 15 minutes to ensure homogeneity.
The homogenized mixture was placed in a Teflon-coated stainless-steel autoclave and heated for hydrothermal synthesis
The autoclave was cooled to room temperature to allow the synthesized material to solidify and form crystals
The powders were removed and dried to remove residual water and impurities
